description_long: This object is a long, cylindrical item with a dark shaft and a flared, ornate end that resembles a spiked or petal-like structure. The shaft appears to be made of metal with a weathered, possibly bronze or iron finish, and features some greenish patina, suggesting age or exposure to the elements. The flared end is composed of several thick, rounded protrusions radiating outward, giving it a robust and heavy appearance. Decorative bands and details are visible along the shaft, adding to its intricate design. The overall form is solid and utilitarian, with no visible strings, frets, or tuning mechanisms typical of a guitar. The object is more reminiscent of a ceremonial mace or club than a musical instrument.
description: A long, dark metallic object with a spiked, ornate end and decorative bands, resembling a ceremonial mace rather than a guitar.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shaft and the flared, spiked end.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the radial, spiked structure of the flared end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the shaft and ornate end, with decorative details visible.
description_view_3: View from the opposite end, showing the rounded base and the spiked end from behind.
